The Python snippet `total = sum(prices) * 1.19` calculates the sum of a list of prices and multiplies it by 1.19 (which represents a 19% tax). In ARO, this would be translated into a feature set that uses the `Compute` action to perform the calculation.

```aro
(Calculate: Tax-Inclusive Total) {
    Extract the <prices> from the <request: body>.
    Compute the <sum> with sum of <prices>.
    Compute the <total> with <sum> * 1.19.
    Return an<OK: status> with the <total>.
}
```

```aro
    Reduce the <sub: Float> from the <prices> with sum().
    Compute the <total> from <sub> * 1.19.

}
```
